justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Atomic acceptance solved via Redis Lua script (equivalent to SET NX) with detailed explanation of why WATCH/MULTI/EXEC and database approaches fail. (2) In-memory Redis state recommended with explicit durability vs latency trade-off (first trade-off entry). (3) Fallback auto-assignment on timer expiry designed via Timeout Scheduler with sorted set. (4) Hot key risk addressed via recommendation to use single hash slot and dedicated Redis instance. (5) Durability vs latency trade-off is a primary trade-off entry with alternatives considered.

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough treatment of atomic acceptance — Lua script chosen over SETNX, with clear reasoning why optimistic locking (WATCH/MULTI) fails under contention
Durability trade-off explicitly called out: Redis AOF with fsync-every-second limits loss to ~1s of windows, accepted as reasonable for 15s lifecycle
Fallback auto-assignment well-designed with sorted set timer, ZPOPMIN for safe multi-poller competition, and clear event flow
Hot key / Redis scalability addressed via dedicated instance recommendation and single hash slot for Lua atomicity
Goes beyond rubric with excellent pitfalls-to-avoid section covering TOCTOU races, client-side expiry countdown, and cache isolation
Clock skew risk addressed via expiresAt in payload and client-side early disable
Scale analysis grounded in concrete numbers (500 windows, 30 couriers, <100K ops/sec needed vs 500K+ capacity)
